title: Agent schedule entry
description: One entry per scheduled agent. Declares the fire time and the deployment facts that decide where it can run. The catalog is the human view of the same cadence; this is the executable view the harness consumes.

agent.description: The agent's run id, matching the catalog, e.g. inbox-usps.

skill.description: Repo-relative path to the SKILL.md the run executes.

cron.description: Five-field cron in operator-local time: minute hour day-of-month month day-of-week. Multiple fire times are separate entries or a comma list per field.

timezone.description: Resolved from the operator's local timezone override, not the host clock, so travel never shifts a fire.

catchup.description: The freshness bucket sys-catchup-controller uses to decide whether a missed slot is worth re-firing.

runtime.description: Which deployment lane fires it. routine = managed cloud (no laptop); desktop-task / device-box = must run on the device the data lives on; ci-cron = CI schedule; gateway = self-hosted 24/7 gateway.

connectors.description: Scope-tagged data the run touches, e.g. gmail:read. Decides cloud-ready vs device-bound.

owns.description: The single field the agent may write, or nothing. Mirrors the catalog's Owns column and the lane fence.

delivery.description: Where the run's output goes, e.g. chat, sms, vault-file, calendar-event.
